readme: Custom Elements Manifest generated by Stencil

declarations.description: Buttons provide a clickable element, which can be used in forms, or anywhere that needs simple, standard button functionality.
declarations.name: Button

members.name: color
members.description: The color to use from your application's color palette.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button.

members.name: expand
members.description: Set to 'full' for a full-width button or to 'block' for a full-width button with rounded corners.

members.name: fill
members.description: Set to 'clear' for a transparent button, to 'outline' for a button with a border, or to 'solid'.

members.name: href
members.description: Contains a URL or a URL fragment that the hyperlink points to.

members.name: size
members.description: The button size.

members.name: strong
members.description: If true, activates a button with a heavier font weight.

members.name: type
members.description: The type of the button.

members.name: setFocus
members.description: Sets focus on the native button element. Use this method instead of the global button.focus().

events.name: ionFocus
events.description: Emitted when the button has focus.

events.name: ionBlur
events.description: Emitted when the button loses focus.

slots.description: Content is placed between the named slots if provided without a slot.

slots.name: icon-only
slots.description: Should be used on an icon in a button that has no text.

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content is placed to the left of the button text in LTR, and to the right in RTL.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content is placed to the right of the button text in LTR, and to the left in RTL.

cssParts.name: native
cssParts.description: The native HTML button or anchor element that wraps all child elements.

declarations.description: The input component is a wrapper to the HTML input element with custom styling and additional functionality.
declarations.name: Input

members.name: color
members.description: The color to use from your application's color palette.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the input.

members.name: label
members.description: The visible label associated with the input.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Instructional text that shows before the input has a value.

members.name: readonly
members.description: If true, the user cannot modify the value.

members.name: required
members.description: If true, the user must fill in a value before submitting a form.

members.name: type
members.description: The type of control to display.

members.name: value
members.description: The value of the input.

members.name: getInputElement
members.description: Returns the native input element used under the hood.

members.name: setFocus
members.description: Sets focus on the native input in ion-input. Use this method instead of the global input.focus().

events.name: ionChange
events.description: The ionChange event is fired when the user modifies the input's value.

events.name: ionInput
events.description: The ionInput event is fired each time the user modifies the input's value.

events.name: ionFocus
events.description: Emitted when the input has focus.

events.name: ionBlur
events.description: Emitted when the input loses focus.

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content to display at the leading edge of the input.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content to display at the trailing edge of the input.

cssProperties.name: --placeholder-color
cssProperties.description: Color of the input placeholder text.

declarations.description: Badges are inline block elements that usually appear near another element. Typically they are used to attract attention to some new or unread content.
declarations.name: Badge

members.name: color
members.description: The color to use from your application's color palette.

slots.description: Content is placed between the opening and closing tags.

cssProperties.name: --padding-end
cssProperties.description: Right padding if direction is left-to-right, and left padding if direction is right-to-left of the badge.

cssProperties.name: --padding-start
cssProperties.description: Left padding if direction is left-to-right, and right padding if direction is right-to-left of the badge.
